decoder_heading: Privacy Policy
ident_heading: Privacy Policy
heading_content_part: 16 Place de l’Université, 1348 Louvain-la-Neuve (Belgium)Email address:[email protected]Company number:0876.816.058

decoder_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
ident_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
heading_content_part: What is the purpose of this document?Speaky is committed to respecting the privacy of Users when collecting and processing Users’ personal data in connection with the Services. Speaky therefore makes every effort to protect the confidentiality of the personal data collected and to comply with applicable data protection laws, including the European Regulation 2016/679, known as the General Data Protection Regulation or, in short, GDPR.

decoder_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
ident_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
heading_content_part: This policy regarding the processing of personal data (hereafter referred to as the“Privacy Policy”) details Speaky’s practices with regard to the collection and processing of personal data (hereafter referred to as“Data”) following consultation of the Site, requesting information and executing the Services from the Site or Platform or via any other electronic means of communication. By “processing of personal data”, we mean any handling of data that can identify you as a natural person.

decoder_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
ident_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
heading_content_part: Please note that the data collected by Speaky in the context of a study on language knowledge is anonymised and is solely intended for the production of anonymous global statistics. This data is not subject to any individual processing and is therefore not subject to the Privacy Policy.

decoder_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
ident_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
heading_content_part: The personal data that we collect is specifically defined in the section below, which we invite you to read carefully. Speaky is committed to respecting your privacy and providing you with as much information as possible to allow you to control what happens to your Data. You will therefore find below further information about the Data we collect, why we collect it, how long we keep it, your privacy rights and how you can exercise them.

decoder_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
ident_heading: What is the purpose of this document?
heading_content_part: Speaky may modify this Privacy Policy periodically, in particular in the event of legislative changes. We therefore invite you to always consult the latest version of the Privacy Policy on our website https://www.speaky.com/privacy-policy/.

decoder_heading: What personal data do we collect?
ident_heading: What personal data do we collect?
heading_content_part: What personal data do we collect?You will find below the different types of Data that we collect, which can be collected cumulatively:The Data collected automatically at the time of each access to the Site by any User:IP addressDate and time of access to the sitePages browsedType of browser usedThe platform and/or operating system installed on the device (computer, tablet, smartphone)The search engine and keywords used to find the site

decoder_heading: What personal data do we collect?
ident_heading: What personal data do we collect?
heading_content_part: Data communicated to Speaky by the Interested Parties that enable us to contact you and perform the Services (login, gender, first name, last name, email address, date of birth, study language, country)Data related to your Speaky Services ordersData collected by the Interested Parties when they contact our customer service department (in particular by email) in order to offer the best Services possible (for the execution of the Services that concern you or as part of pre-contractual measures)

decoder_heading: What personal data do we collect?
ident_heading: What personal data do we collect?
heading_content_part: The information collected automatically when a User uses the Site or the Platform:Operating activities (which are recorded in a designated file in the form of a “log”), including the User’s login, the features used, the time of execution or “timestamp”, and the workstation used via the User’s IP addressConnections to the platformSpeaky uses your Data only to the extent necessary for the purposes for which it was obtained as described in the section below.

decoder_heading: What personal data do we collect?
ident_heading: What personal data do we collect?
heading_content_part: Please note that as the use of Speaky is not intended for children under the age of 16, we do not intentionally collect personal information from children under the age of 16 without the explicit authorisation of a parent or guardian. In the event that information about children under the age of 16 has been inadvertently collected by us, we will take steps to delete the Data as soon as possible, unless we are required by law to store that information.

decoder_heading: What personal data do we collect?
ident_heading: What personal data do we collect?
heading_content_part: We also use cookies – please see our Cookie Policy section for further information.

decoder_heading: For what purposes and on what legal basis do we use your Data?
ident_heading: For what purposes and on what legal basis do we use your Data?
heading_content_part: For what purposes and on what legal basis do we use your Data?Speaky uses your Data for various purposes; We only use the data that is essential to achieve the intended purpose and when necessary:In the context of the preparation of a contract, its performance or its dissolution. For example, to provide you with the requested Services and respond to questions you have about the Services.

decoder_heading: For what purposes and on what legal basis do we use your Data?
ident_heading: For what purposes and on what legal basis do we use your Data?
heading_content_part: To conform with the legal and regulatory provisions to which Speaky is subject. For example, in connection with tax report filings and accounting obligations.On the basis of our legitimate interests, ensuring in such cases to balance these with respect for your privacy. We may thus use the Data of the Interested Parties for the purpose of informing, advertising, marketing and improving Speaky’s products and services. Specifically, this allows us to:Send you our Newsletter

decoder_heading: For what purposes and on what legal basis do we use your Data?
ident_heading: For what purposes and on what legal basis do we use your Data?
heading_content_part: Offer you new or similar products, services, or special campaigns that are likely to be of interest to you (by mail, telephone, email or via social networks)Send you promotional offers about similar products and servicesFinally, Speaky may collect and use Data with your consent if it is to send you promotional messages, provided that you are not yet listed as an Interested Party. In this context, you may at any time request to unsubscribe to these promotional messages.

decoder_heading: To whom can we transmit your Data?
ident_heading: To whom can we transmit your Data?
heading_content_part: To whom can we transmit your Data?We only transmit your Data to third parties when:It is necessary for the proper execution of our ServicesIt is necessary to meet a legal obligation

decoder_heading: To whom can we transmit your Data?
ident_heading: To whom can we transmit your Data?
heading_content_part: In order to execute our Services, we may subcontract, in whole or in part, some of our tasks to technical subcontractors, for example to an IT company with a view to hosting the Site and storing the Data and backup copies. We ensure that subcontractors handle your Data in the same manner we do; that is, in a safe, respectful and responsible way, and we provide adequate contractual guarantees to this effect. Speaky therefore requires its subcontractors to comply with data protection legislation and to provide sufficient guarantees regarding the implementation of appropriate technical and organisational measures, so that the use of the data meets the requirements of applicable data protection legislation and guarantees the protection of your rights.

decoder_heading: What are your rights in relation to your Data?
ident_heading: What are your rights in relation to your Data?
heading_content_part: What are your rights in relation to your Data?Depending on the processing and the legal basis, there are a number of possibilities available to you to keep control over your Data:Right to access your DataRight to amend your DataRight to object to the processing of your DataRight to limit the processing of your dataRight to have your Data deletedRight to the portability of your DataRight to withdraw your consentTo exercise your rights, please send an email to[email protected].

decoder_heading: For how long do we keep your Data?
ident_heading: For how long do we keep your Data?
heading_content_part: For how long do we keep your Data?

decoder_heading: For how long do we keep your Data?
ident_heading: For how long do we keep your Data?
heading_content_part: We will not retain your Data beyond the time necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it is collected. Speaky may, however, use the contact information of Interested Parties to identify and inform them of new Speaky products and promotions for a period of 2 years, unless the Interested Parties have unsubscribed from these promotional messages. At the end of the storage periods described above, Data will be deleted or made anonymous, except insofar as its storage is a legal obligation, specifically in case of compliance with the obligation to keep documents for accounting and tax purposes.

decoder_heading: How do we protect your Data?
ident_heading: How do we protect your Data?
heading_content_part: How do we protect your Data?

decoder_heading: How do we protect your Data?
ident_heading: How do we protect your Data?
heading_content_part: We have developed appropriate technical and organisational security rules in order to prevent the destruction, loss, falsification, modification, unauthorised access, accidental disclosure to third parties and any other unauthorised handling of Data. For example, Speaky synchronises databases containing User or Client Data securely on three different physical supports. In order to protect the entire system against viruses and security breaches, system software and application software packages are updated as soon as a security update is available. A firewall is installed on each server and is configured by our network administrator to protect against any external attack. Access to the servers is also restricted by private-public key, with the private key being encrypted (DES 256). Finally, the individual computer of each Speaky employee is equipped with an automatically updated and professional anti-virus software. In addition, developers or employees working on the infrastructure are equipped with a computer that has a fully encrypted hard disk. In the event of a data leak with adverse consequences for your Data, you are personally notified under the circumstances and within the time limits as stipulated by law. To ensure the security of your Data, we advise that you:

decoder_heading: How do we protect your Data?
ident_heading: How do we protect your Data?
heading_content_part: Do not disclose your password to third partiesChange your password regularlyMake sure that access to your computer/tablet/smartphone is secureYou are solely responsible for your Client Account and the consequences of its use, as well as for any information of any kind that you communicate to Speaky. Speaky is not liable in the event of damage caused because of erroneous, incomplete or fraudulent information that you provide to us.

decoder_heading: What do you need to know about third-party sites?
ident_heading: What do you need to know about third-party sites?
heading_content_part: What do you need to know about third-party sites?Our website may contain links to third-party sites, and some of our Services may grant you access to third-party services (such as social networks). We have no control over how third-party sites and services handle your Data. We do not review third party sites and services, and we are not responsible for those third-party sites and services or their practices regarding the protection of your Data. We invite you to carefully read the data handling policies of third-party sites that you access from our Site.

decoder_heading: How to contact us?
ident_heading: How to contact us?
heading_content_part: How to contact us?If you wish to exercise your rights or if you have questions regarding the handling of your Data, you may contact us by sending an email to:[email protected].Do not hesitate to contact us should you consider that we have not treated your Data appropriately. We will do our best to follow up your request as quickly as possible. Should you remain unsatisfied with our response, you can also file a complaint with the data protection authority:Address:Rue de la Presse, 35, 1000 Brussels, Belgium

decoder_heading: What is a cookie policy?
ident_heading: What is a cookie policy?
heading_content_part: What is a cookie policy?This cookie policy aims to provide information that is as clear and complete as possible about the cookies we use and their purpose. We also invite you to read our Privacy Policy to learn about the rules that apply to the protection of personal data.

decoder_heading: What is a cookie policy?
ident_heading: What is a cookie policy?
heading_content_part: A cookie is a small data file stored on the hard drive of your computer, tablet or smartphone when you visit a website. The cookie is placed on your device by the server that hosts the website. The server recognises your device when you return to the site in question because of a cookie’s unique ID number. There are different types of cookies, categorised based on origin, function and lifetime.

decoder_heading: What types of cookies do we use?
ident_heading: What types of cookies do we use?
heading_content_part: What types of cookies do we use?TheSiteand thePlatformprimarily use “third-party cookies”. This type of cookie can only be read by our Services.However, certain pages of our Site and Platform may use external services, which also define their own cookies, called third party cookies. This is why you must accept cookies from these external services in order to be able to consult some of our pages.We use our3 types of third-party cookiesto:Save visitors’ preferences

decoder_heading: How to Manage Cookies
ident_heading: How to Manage Cookies
heading_content_part: How to Manage CookiesYou canmanage and/or deletecookies as you wish. To find out more, consultaboutcookies.org. You can delete all the cookies already stored on your computer and configure the majority of browsers so that they block them. However, in this case, you may need to indicate certain preferences each time you visit a site and note that certain services and functionalities may not be accessible.
